note: Interview/speech-screenshot provenance task. The executor gets a video frame (sources/screenshot.jpg) with no channel watermark, no caption, no on-screen title. They must visually identify the speaker + occasion and trace it to the canonical original video. A plain search of a frame can't be done; reverse-image + recognition is needed, and the answer must be the OFFICIAL upload, not a reaction/clip channel.

frame_origin.source: YouTube video UF8uR6Z6KLc (Stanford official channel) — auto thumbnail frame, no overlay text
frame_origin.what_it_shows: a balding man in academic regalia (dark gown, magenta/red hood) speaking at a lectern with a microphone, outdoors/auditorium with red banners behind

canonical_answer.speaker: Steve Jobs
canonical_answer.occasion: Stanford University 2005 Commencement Address ('Stay Hungry. Stay Foolish.')
canonical_answer.also_acceptable_canonical: Stanford News official transcript/page for the 2005 commencement address (news.stanford.edu)

scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: canonical = the official Stanford upload of the address (YouTube UF8uR6Z6KLc) or Stanford's own published transcript/page. Reaction/clip/re-upload channels and news aggregators are secondary.
scoring_anchors.anti_secondary_source: stopping at a random re-upload, a 'top moments' clip channel, or a news article without identifying the official Stanford source caps the score
scoring_anchors.tolerance: Accept identifying speaker=Steve Jobs + occasion=Stanford 2005 commencement as the core. Full credit requires reaching the official Stanford video (UF8uR6Z6KLc) OR Stanford's own transcript page. If the executor identifies it correctly but only reaches a non-official re-upload, give partial credit.
